'''Ricky Bibey''' (22 September 1981 – 16 July 2022) was an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}}. He made his professional debut in 2001 for [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref name="Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order">{{Cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|title=Heritage Numbers – In Debut Order|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327102545/http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|url-status=dead}}</ref> and went on to play for [[Leigh Centurions]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] before being forced to retire in 2012 due to injury. He also represented [[England national rugby league team|England A]], making three appearances for the team in 2002.

==Background==
Bibey was born in [[Leigh, Greater Manchester]], England.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}} He played junior [[rugby league]] at [[Leigh East A.R.L.F.C.|Leigh East]], and in 1999, he was selected in the Young Lions squad that played a Test series against Australia Schoolboys.<ref>{{cite web |title=Lions Get Ready To Roar |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5299138.lions-get-ready-to-roar/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=9 December 1999}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Bibey made his professional debut for [[Wigan Warriors]] in May 2001 against [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. However, he was then sent off in his second appearance for the club in a 30–31 defeat against [[Salford City Reds]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Salford push Endacott a step closer to the gallows |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2001/may/14/rugbyleague.wiganwarriors |website=The Guardian |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=14 May 2001}}</ref> Following the sacking of Wigan coach [[Frank Endacott]], he struggled to regain his place in the team, and made just four appearances during the 2001 season.

In 2002, Bibey featured more regularly in the first team, playing 26 games for Wigan during the season, including a substitute appearance in the 21–12 win against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[2002 Challenge Cup]] final.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan shock favourites Saints |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/1952991.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=27 April 2002}}</ref> Shortly after the cup final, Bibey was involved in a car crash, but was not seriously injured.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Richards |first1=Martin |title=O'Neill repays faith by inspiring Wigan |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2002/may/02/rugbyleague.wiganwarriors |website=The Guardian |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=2 May 2002}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Hand injury rules Long out of Test in Australia |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/hand-injury-rules-long-out-of-test-in-australia-9182948.html |website=The Independent |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=3 May 2002}}</ref> At the end of the season, he was selected in the [[England national rugby league team|England A]] squad which toured the Southern Hemisphere, and played in matches against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] and [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].

In July 2003, Bibey joined his hometown club [[Leigh Centurions]] on a one-month loan,<ref>{{cite web |title=Rhinos' charge goes on with signing of Bai |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2003/jul/25/rugbyleague.leedsrhinos |website=The Guardian |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=25 July 2003}}</ref> which was later extended until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bibey boost for Centurions |url=https://www.theboltonnews.co.uk/news/5888284.bibey-boost-for-centurions/ |website=The Bolton News |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=15 August 2003}}</ref>

Bibey is one of the few players to win a Challenge Cup as both a Wigan Warriors and a St. Helens player as part of Wigan Warriors' 2002 Challenge Cup-winning squad and then in St Helens' 2004 squad. Bibey was also a member of the Leigh Centurions side that won the Northern Rail Cup in 2006 but was injured when the Leigh Centurions won the same competition again in 2011.

He was forced to retire in May 2012 as a result of an [[achilles tendon]] injury.<ref name="Injury Forces Bibey To Retire">{{cite web|url=http://www.leighreporter.co.uk/sport/leigh-centurions/injury-forces-bibey-to-retire-1-4586880|title=Injury Forces Bibey To Retire|publisher=leighreporter.co.uk|date=25 May 2012|accessdate=25 May 2012}}{{Dead link|date=January 2026 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}</ref>

After hanging up his boots, Bibey coached junior sides at Oldham St Anne's ARLFC.<ref>[http://www.saints.org.uk/saints/player.php?num=16594 Saints Heritage Society Biography of Ricky Bibey]</ref>

==Death==
On the morning of 16 July 2022, Bibey was found dead in a hotel room in [[Florence]]. He was aged 40.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Rowan |first1=Claudia |title=Former rugby league player Ricky Bibey 'found dead in Florence hotel room' |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/florence-manchester-italian-lancashire-yorkshire-b2125081.html |website=The Independent |access-date=17 July 2022 |date=17 July 2022}}</ref> A seriously injured 43-year-old woman, thought to be his partner, was also found at the scene.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-62195043|title=Rugby player Ricky Bibey, 40, found dead in Italy – reports|work=BBC News |date=17 July 2022|accessdate=17 July 2022}}</ref> The cause of death is attributable to the intake of cocaine which would have caused a heart attack.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ex-rugbista morto in hotel a Firenze, parla la compagna: "Quella notte mi ha aggredito e violentato" |url=https://www.tgcom24.mediaset.it/cronaca/ex-rugbista-morto-in-hotel-a-firenze-parla-la-compagna-quella-notte-mi-ha-aggredito-e-violentato_53108106-202202k.shtml |access-date=2 August 2022 |website=Tgcom24 |date=2 August 2022 |language=IT}}</ref> Bibey reportedly suffered the heart attack while beating his partner.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/rugby-league-player-ricky-bibey-died-in-florence-hotel-room-while-beating-lover-805cflw5w | title=Rugby league player Ricky Bibey died in Florence hotel room 'while beating lover' }}</ref>
